title: Obadiah (Codex Vaticanus)
source: Codex Vaticanus (4th c.) via Vatican Library IIIF + KJV
description: The book of Obadiah from Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209, 4th century AD), considered by many scholars the most important manuscript of the Greek Bible. Folio scans via Vatican Library IIIF (digi.vatlib.it). English from the King James Version (1611, public domain).
introduction: Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209, 4th century AD) is widely regarded as the single most important manuscript of the Greek Bible. It has been in the Vatican Library since at least 1475. Along with Codex Sinaiticus, it is one of the two oldest near-complete copies of the Bible in existence.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>The vision of Obadiah. Thus saith the Lord GOD concerning Edom; We have heard a rumour from the LORD, and an ambassador is sent among the heathen, Arise ye, and let us rise up against her in battle.
